method_atom: A large-system (thermodynamic-limit) statistical-mechanics analysis of sequential, sample-by-sample adaptation in which an adaptive unit incrementally updates its internal parameters from a stream of examples generated by a slowly drifting, non-stationary target rule; a variational method derives the exact best-achievable tracking/prediction performance and constructs an update rule, and the rule's dependence on unobservable quantities is closed by building estimators of those quantities to yield a highly adaptive algorithm, benchmarked against the optimal bound.
object_atom: A feed-forward neural network (the student) that learns online from examples produced by a time-dependent teacher network of the same architecture, i.e. tracking a drifting concept or classification rule.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the statistical-mechanics/variational treatment of online adaptation to a drifting target with estimator-based closure of unobservable quantities; the domain-specific part is the feed-forward student-teacher neural-network learning setup.
